title: fenelonia bracteata
intro: Fenelonia bracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the lesser bugloss. The plant's leaves, flowers, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Fenelonia bracteata include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues due to its carminative and anti-spasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Fenelonia bracteata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support respiratory health and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
